steps.title: Match the civic importance
steps.content: The passage argues that public understanding of history is central to understanding state politics and that historical issues should not be left only to specialists. The blank must support the conclusion that knowledge of history matters broadly to a functioning citizenry.

steps.title: Reject unrelated adjectives
steps.content: B means fair or impartial, but the sentence is not about fairness. C means ordinary or widespread, which does not explain why history should not be left only to historians. D means impossible to obtain, while the passage assumes citizens can and should understand history. The answer is A.
